title: IAA unveils Corporate Plan following rebrand
body: "The Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) has today published its Corporate Plan for 2025 to 2027, following the launch of its new identity.  To support the delivery of the Corporate Plan, an interim Chief Executive Officer (CEO) has been appointed. Heather Laing brings extensive knowledge and experience in the immigration sector; her expertise will strengthen the organisation’s leadership team and play a crucial role in the successful implementation of the plan’s objectives.  John Tuckett, Immigration Servies Commissioner, said:   The publication of our Corporate Plan, coupled with Heather Laing’s appointment, strengthens the IAA’s ability to achieve our ambitious vision.    Our rebrand signals a new chapter to achieve our strategic goals by redefining our vision, driving transformation, and reinforcing our commitment to regulating the immigration sector with the advice seeker at the core of our work.   Heather Laing, interim CEO, said:   I join the IAA at a pivotal moment. Today marks an exciting day for the organisation and our service users. Our Corporate Plan provides a clear roadmap for transformation, reinforcing our role in regulating the immigration sector.    Together with our talented team, I am dedicated to achieving the goals set out in this ambitious strategy and driving forward this enhanced agenda.   The 2025/2027 Corporate Plan outlines the key focus on transforming the IAA into a high-performing, outward-focused organisation, ensuring that advice is easily accessible to seekers while disrupting the provision of illegal advice by unregulated individuals. Key objectives include:   Regulation: Ensuring compliance with established standards for registered immigration advisers Enforcement: Disrupting illegal advice-giving activities that undermine the integrity of the immigration system Promoting best practice: Developing the immigration advice sector, working in partnership with advisers and stakeholders   Read the full 2025/2027 Corporate Plan "
